mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 7.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies earnings revision momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, with a cumulative information coefficient below 0.02 over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost pressures and supports corporate profit margins, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC turns persistently negative during supply-side expansions, indicating that output growth consistently precedes equity drawdowns over the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP accelerates corporate revenue expectations, leading to upward earnings revisions that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, rejecting the earnings revision channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading exceeding 2.5 percent annualized triggers fears of premature monetary tightening and higher real yields, which compress valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where CPI exceeds the threshold, disproving the real yield compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stagnant unemployment rate indicates labor force participation collapse rather than genuine demand, signaling structural weakness that depresses equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in the majority of observations, indicating that labor market participation dynamics do not negatively impact equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts peaking above 1.0 million units while retail sales growth decelerates signals consumer spending exhaustion, which precedes earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate above 50% in walk-forward testing, rejecting the consumer exhaustion premise.
